seed_problem: Assign frequencies to transmitters minimizing total interference conflicts under a channel budget; checker scores baseline_conflicts/achieved.
mutation: composite: change the goal from frequency assignment to weighted minimum-conflict k-coloring of a museum overlap graph (minimize total annoyance weight of monochromatic edges), and generalize the input from a geometric interference graph to an arbitrary dense heavy-tailed weighted (multi)graph so optimal k-coloring is NP-hard and round-robin / one-pass greedy / local-search strategies diverge.
title: Whispering Halls: Audio-Beacon Channel Planning
strategies: channel-cycling baseline: gallery g gets channel ((g-1) mod k)+1, ignoring the overlap structure entirely
one-pass min-conflict greedy: assign galleries in id order, each to the channel adding the least annoyance with already-assigned overlapping neighbors
iterated local search: greedy init then repeatedly move each gallery to its least-annoying channel given the full current assignment, until convergence
weight-aware randomized restarts: multiple perturbed local-search runs keeping the plan with minimum total annoyance
